name: D — Yield-protection pattern: high stats, school history shows admits skewing lower

expected.scatterplotPosition: above admitted cluster but in the cluster of denied high-stat applicants

rationale: WUSTL is yield-protected. Standard model already caps stat multiplier at 1.0 for yield-protected schools. Adding the school-history blend on a 1/9 admit rate (~11%) should pull final chance down further despite strong stats; the percentile-multiplier S-curve will boost the school chance toward the upper end (user is +2.75σ above admitted SAT mean) but the raw 11% admit rate is still much lower than national RD baseline. Key validation: the experiment should NOT mask this with the percentile multiplier — denials at 1550+ are already in the data.
